page: UpCloud STARTER-1xCPU-2GB is an entry-level virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single shared vCPU from an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with x86_64 architecture, operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2.0 GB of system memory, 20 GB of local SSD storage, and one complimentary public IPv4 address. The CPU contains 16 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, SHA_NI, and AES cryptography.

Benchmark results demonstrate a stark contrast between single-threaded compute and multi-threaded or memory-bound operations. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, rank in the top tier. However, due to the single shared vCPU limitation, multi-core performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is also constrained, with a PassMark memory score in the bottom 10% and cached read-only memory bandwidth at 216.15 GB/sec. Large block memory write bandwidth is average at 31.63 GB/sec.

This server represents a budget-oriented tier where resource density is low, making it a cost-efficient option for lightweight, non-continuous workloads. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation and limited 2.0 GB memory capacity, which restricts its utility for resource-intensive applications. It is suitable for basic web serving, lightweight single-threaded tasks, small-scale development environments, and simple utility scripts that do not require sustained multi-core processing or high memory throughput.
description: UpCloud STARTER-1xCPU-2GB is an entry-level virtual machine utilizing the KVM hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 9575F processor. It features 1 shared vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 20 GB of local SSD storage. While single-threaded compute benchmarks, such as gzip compression and single-core stress-ng, rank in the top 10%, its overall multi-core and memory performance fall into the lower percentiles due to the single-core shared resource model. This server is a cost-effective option for lightweight workloads, basic web serving, and development environments that do not demand high memory bandwidth or parallel processing.
og_description: UpCloud STARTER-1xCPU-2GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 20 GB of SSD storage, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server with strong single-threaded compute performance for lightweight utility workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: 1 shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPU and 2.0 GB of system memory
20 GB of local SSD storage and 1 public IPv4 address included
Top-tier single-threaded CPU and compression benchmark performance
Constrained multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities
Best suited for lightweight web serving, utility scripts, and development
